label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. The question is asking for the date one week from the last day of 1899, which would be December 31, 1899. We need to add seven days to that date and see what the new date would be, then match it with one of the given options. First, let's confirm the starting date. If today is the last day of 1899, that means today is December 31, 1899. Adding one week (which is seven days) to that date would take us to January 7, 1900, because the next day after December 31, 1899 is January 1, 1900. So adding seven days would be: December 31, 1899 + 1 day = January 1, 1900 January 1, 1900 + 6 more days = January 7, 1900 So one week from December 31, 1899 would be January 7, 1900, which is written in MM/DD/YYYY format as 01/07/1900.
